* Response size limits middleware for Hono.\n *\n * OWASP A01:2021 - Broken Access Control (DoS Prevention)\n * OWASP A04:2021 - Insecure Design (Resource Limits)\n *\n * Protects against DoS attacks via large response payloads\n * that could exhaust bandwidth, memory, or cause client-side issues.\n *\n * Attack scenarios:\n * - Malicious backend or data source returns extremely large responses\n * - Accidentally large responses from bugs cause service degradation\n * - Memory exhaustion from buffering large responses\n * - Bandwidth exhaustion affecting other clients\n *\n * This middleware limits response body size and streams responses\n * when possible to avoid buffering large payloads in memory.\n */\n\nimport type { MiddlewareHandler } from \"hono\";\nimport { securityLogger } from \"./security-logging.js\";\n\n/**\n * Response size limit options.\n */\ninterface ResponseSizeLimitOptions {\n  /** Maximum response body size in bytes (default: 10MB) */\n  maxResponseSize?: number;\n  /** Maximum response body size for JSON responses (default: 1MB) */\n  maxJsonSize?: number;\n  /** Stream responses larger than this threshold (default: 100KB) */\n  streamThreshold?: number;\n  /** Skip validation for these paths (default: []) */\n  excludePaths?: string[];\n  /** Whether to compress large responses (default: true) */\n  compressLargeResponses?: boolean;\n}\n\n/** Default options */\nconst DEFAULT_OPTIONS: Required<Omit<ResponseSizeLimitOptions, \"excludePaths\">> = {\n  maxResponseSize: 10 * 1024 * 1024, // 10MB\n  maxJsonSize: 1024 * 1024, // 1MB\n  streamThreshold: 100 * 1024, // 100KB\n  compressLargeResponses: true,\n};\n\n/**\n * Estimate response size from headers.\n *\n * Checks Content-Length header if present.\n */\nfunction estimateResponseSize(response: Response): number | undefined {\n  const contentLength = response.headers.get(\"Content-Length\");\n  if (contentLength) {\n    const size = parseInt(contentLength, 10);\n    return isNaN(size) ? undefined : size;\n  }\n  return undefined;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if response should be streamed based on content type and size.\n */\nfunction shouldStream(\n  contentType: string | null,\n  estimatedSize: number | undefined,\n  options: Required<Omit<ResponseSizeLimitOptions, \"excludePaths\">>\n): boolean {\n  if (!estimatedSize) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // Stream if size exceeds threshold\n  if (estimatedSize > options.streamThreshold) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // Stream certain content types regardless of size\n  if (contentType) {\n    const streamTypes = [\n      \"video/\",\n      \"audio/\",\n      \"application/octet-stream\",\n      \"application/pdf\",\n      \"application/zip\",\n    ];\n    if (streamTypes.some((type) => contentType.startsWith(type))) {\n      return true;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\n/**\n * Response size limit middleware.\n *\n * Validates response body size and applies appropriate limits\n * based on content type. Streams large responses to avoid buffering.\n */\nexport function responseSizeLimits(options: ResponseSizeLimitOptions = {}): MiddlewareHandler {\n  const {\n    maxResponseSize,\n    maxJsonSize,\n    streamThreshold,\n    excludePaths = [],\n    compressLargeResponses,\n  } = {\n    ...DEFAULT_OPTIONS,\n    ...options,\n  };\n\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    // Skip if path is excluded\n    if (excludePaths.some((path) => c.req.path.startsWith(path))) {\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // Store original json method\n    const originalJson = c.json.bind(c);\n\n    // Override json method to check response size\n    c.json = (data: unknown, status = 200, headers = {}) => {\n      const response = originalJson(data, status, headers);\n\n      // Estimate JSON size\n      const jsonString = JSON.stringify(data);\n      const jsonSize = new TextEncoder().encode(jsonString).length;\n\n      // Check JSON size limit\n      if (jsonSize > maxJsonSize) {\n        securityLogger.logBlockedAttack(c, \"json_response_size_limit\", {\n          actualSize: jsonSize,\n          maxSize: maxJsonSize,\n        });\n        return originalJson(\n          {\n            error: \"Response too large\",\n            maxSize: maxJsonSize,\n            actualSize: jsonSize,\n          },\n          413\n        );\n      }\n\n      return response;\n    };\n\n    await next();\n\n    // Check response size after handler executes\n    const response = c.res;\n    const contentType = response.headers.get(\"Content-Type\");\n    const estimatedSize = estimateResponseSize(response);\n\n    // Determine appropriate size limit based on content type\n    let maxSize = maxResponseSize;\n    if (contentType?.includes(\"application/json\")) {\n      maxSize = maxJsonSize;\n    }\n\n    // Check if size limit is exceeded\n    if (estimatedSize && estimatedSize > maxSize) {\n      securityLogger.logBlockedAttack(c, \"response_size_limit\", {\n        actualSize: estimatedSize,\n        maxSize,\n        contentType,\n      });\n\n      // Override response with error\n      c.res = originalJson(\n        {\n          error: \"Response too large\",\n          maxSize,\n          actualSize: estimatedSize,\n        },\n        413\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Add compression hint for large responses\n    if (estimatedSize && estimatedSize > streamThreshold && compressLargeResponses) {\n      const acceptEncoding = c.req.header(\"Accept-Encoding\");\n      if (acceptEncoding?.includes(\"br\") || acceptEncoding?.includes(\"gzip\")) {\n        // Response will be compressed by compression middleware\n        c.res.headers.set(\"X-Should-Compress\", \"true\");\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Log large responses for monitoring\n    if (estimatedSize && estimatedSize > 1024 * 1024) {\n      // Log responses > 1MB\n      securityLogger.logSecurityEvent(c, \"large_response\", {\n        size: estimatedSize,\n        contentType: contentType || \"unknown\",\n      });\n    }\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a streaming response handler.\n *\n * Use this for responses that should be streamed to avoid buffering.\n */\nexport function createStreamResponse(\n  streamGenerator: () => ReadableStream<Uint8Array>,\n  contentType: string,\n  headers: Record<string, string> = {}\n): Response {\n  return new Response(streamGenerator(), {\n    headers: {\n      \"Content-Type\": contentType,\n      \"Transfer-Encoding\": \"chunked\",\n      \"X-Content-Streamed\": \"true\",\n      ...headers,\n    },\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Paginated response helper.\n *\n * Helps break large responses into paginated chunks.\n */\nexport interface PaginatedResponse<T> {\n  data: T[];\n  pagination: {\n    page: number;\n    pageSize: number;\n    totalCount: number;\n    hasMore: boolean;\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a paginated response from a large dataset.\n */\nexport function createPaginatedResponse<T>(\n  data: T[],\n  page: number,\n  pageSize: number\n): PaginatedResponse<T> {\n  const offset = (page - 1) * pageSize;\n  const paginatedData = data.slice(offset, offset + pageSize);\n  const totalCount = data.length;\n  const hasMore = offset + pageSize < totalCount;\n\n  return {\n    data: paginatedData,\n    pagination: {\n      page,\n      pageSize,\n      totalCount,\n      hasMore,\n    },\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Response payload size estimator utility.\n *\n * Estimates the size of a data payload before sending it as JSON.\n */\nexport function estimatePayloadSize(data: unknown): number {\n  if (data === null || data === undefined) {\n    return 4; // \"null\"\n  }\n\n  if (typeof data === \"string\") {\n    return new TextEncoder().encode(data).length;\n  }\n\n  if (typeof data === \"number\") {\n    return data.toString().length;\n  }\n\n  if (typeof data === \"boolean\") {\n    return data ? 4 : 5; // \"true\" or \"false\"\n  }\n\n  if (Array.isArray(data)) {\n    return data.reduce((sum, item) => sum + estimatePayloadSize(item), 2); // 2 for brackets\n  }\n\n  if (typeof data === \"object\") {\n    let size = 2; // 2 for braces\n    const entries = Object.entries(data);\n    entries.forEach(([key, value], index) => {\n      size += new TextEncoder().encode(key).length + 3; // key + \":\"\n      size += estimatePayloadSize(value);\n      if (index < entries.length - 1) {\n        size += 1; // \",\"\n      }\n    });\n    return size;\n  }\n\n  return 0;\n}\n","numLines":302,"startLine":1,"totalLines":302}}}

* JSON depth protection middleware for Hono.\n *\n * OWASP A01:2021 - Broken Access Control (DoS Prevention)\n * OWASP A03:2021 - Injection (JSON DoS Prevention)\n *\n * Protects against DoS attacks via deeply nested JSON structures\n * that could cause stack overflow or excessive memory usage.\n *\n * Attack scenarios:\n * - Malicious client sends deeply nested JSON to exhaust stack\n * - Large JSON payloads with excessive nesting cause parsing delays\n * - Recursive data structures that never terminate\n *\n * This middleware validates JSON structure depth before full parsing.\n */\n\nimport type { MiddlewareHandler } from \"hono\";\nimport { HTTPException } from \"hono/http-exception\";\nimport { securityLogger } from \"./security-logging.js\";\n\n/**\n * JSON depth protection options.\n */\ninterface JsonDepthProtectionOptions {\n  /** Maximum allowed nesting depth (default: 32) */\n  maxDepth?: number;\n  /** Maximum array length (default: 1000) */\n  maxArrayLength?: number;\n  /** Maximum object keys (default: 100) */\n  maxObjectKeys?: number;\n  /** Skip validation for these paths (default: []) */\n  excludePaths?: string[];\n}\n\n/** Default options */\nconst DEFAULT_OPTIONS: Required<Omit<JsonDepthProtectionOptions, \"excludePaths\">> = {\n  maxDepth: 32,\n  maxArrayLength: 1000,\n  maxObjectKeys: 100,\n};\n\n/**\n * Calculate the depth of a JSON value.\n *\n * Returns the maximum nesting depth of objects and arrays.\n * Primitives (strings, numbers, booleans, null) return 0.\n */\nfunction calculateJsonDepth(value: unknown, currentDepth = 0, options = DEFAULT_OPTIONS): number {\n  // Check if we've exceeded the maximum depth\n  if (currentDepth > options.maxDepth) {\n    return currentDepth;\n  }\n\n  if (Array.isArray(value)) {\n    // Check array length\n    if (value.length > options.maxArrayLength) {\n      return currentDepth + options.maxArrayLength;\n    }\n    // Recursively check array elements\n    let maxChildDepth = currentDepth;\n    for (const item of value) {\n      const childDepth = calculateJsonDepth(item, currentDepth + 1, options);\n      maxChildDepth = Math.max(maxChildDepth, childDepth);\n    }\n    return maxChildDepth;\n  }\n\n  if (value !== null && typeof value === \"object\") {\n    // Check object key count\n    const keys = Object.keys(value);\n    if (keys.length > options.maxObjectKeys) {\n      return currentDepth + options.maxObjectKeys;\n    }\n    // Recursively check object values\n    let maxChildDepth = currentDepth;\n    for (const key of keys) {\n      const childDepth = calculateJsonDepth(\n        (value as Record<string, unknown>)[key],\n        currentDepth + 1,\n        options\n      );\n      maxChildDepth = Math.max(maxChildDepth, childDepth);\n    }\n    return maxChildDepth;\n  }\n\n  // Primitives don't increase depth\n  return currentDepth;\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate JSON structure without full parsing.\n *\n * Uses a simple state machine to track nesting depth\n * while scanning the JSON string character by character.\n * This is more efficient than full parsing for rejection.\n */\nfunction validateJsonStructure(\n  jsonString: string,\n  options: Required<Omit<JsonDepthProtectionOptions, \"excludePaths\">>\n): {\n  valid: boolean;\n  reason?: string;\n  depth?: number;\n} {\n  let depth = 0;\n  let maxDepth = 0;\n  let arrayLength = 0;\n  let objectKeys = 0;\n  let inString = false;\n  let escapeNext = false;\n  let inKey = true; // true when parsing object key, false for value\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < jsonString.length; i++) {\n    const char = jsonString[i];\n\n    // Handle escape sequences\n    if (escapeNext) {\n      escapeNext = false;\n      continue;\n    }\n    if (char === \"\\\\\") {\n      escapeNext = true;\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    // Track string boundaries\n    if (char === '\"') {\n      inString = !inString;\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    // Skip content inside strings\n    if (inString) {\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    // Track nesting depth\n    switch (char) {\n      case \"{\":\n        depth++;\n        maxDepth = Math.max(maxDepth, depth);\n        objectKeys = 0;\n        inKey = true;\n        if (depth > options.maxDepth) {\n          return { valid: false, reason: \"Maximum object depth exceeded\", depth: maxDepth };\n        }\n        break;\n      case \"}\":\n        depth--;\n        inKey = false;\n        break;\n      case \"[\":\n        depth++;\n        maxDepth = Math.max(maxDepth, depth);\n        arrayLength = 0;\n        if (depth > options.maxDepth) {\n          return { valid: false, reason: \"Maximum array depth exceeded\", depth: maxDepth };\n        }\n        break;\n      case \"]\":\n        depth--;\n        if (arrayLength > options.maxArrayLength) {\n          return { valid: false, reason: \"Maximum array length exceeded\" };\n        }\n        break;\n      case \":\":\n        // Transition from key to value in object\n        inKey = false;\n        break;\n      case \",\":\n        // Increment counters for next element\n        if (depth > 0) {\n          const prevChar = jsonString[i - 1];\n          if (prevChar === \"]\" || prevChar === \"}\") {\n            // After an array element or object value\n            if (jsonString[i + 1] === \"{\" || jsonString[i + 1] === \"[\") {\n              // Next is a nested structure, increment appropriate counter\n              const parentStart = jsonString.lastIndexOf(\"{\", i);\n              const arrayParentStart = jsonString.lastIndexOf(\"[\", i);\n              if (arrayParentStart > parentStart) {\n                arrayLength++;\n              } else {\n                objectKeys++;\n              }\n            }\n          }\n        }\n        break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check final counts\n  if (objectKeys > options.maxObjectKeys) {\n    return { valid: false, reason: \"Maximum object keys exceeded\" };\n  }\n\n  return { valid: true, depth: maxDepth };\n}\n\n/**\n * JSON depth protection middleware.\n *\n * Validates JSON request body structure depth and size limits\n * to prevent DoS attacks via malicious JSON payloads.\n */\nexport function jsonDepthProtection(options: JsonDepthProtectionOptions = {}): MiddlewareHandler {\n  const {\n    maxDepth,\n    maxArrayLength,\n    maxObjectKeys,\n    excludePaths = [],\n  } = { ...DEFAULT_OPTIONS, ...options };\n\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    // Skip if path is excluded\n    if (excludePaths.some((path) => c.req.path.startsWith(path))) {\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // Only apply to requests with JSON body\n    const contentType = c.req.header(\"Content-Type\");\n    if (!contentType?.includes(\"application/json\")) {\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    try {\n      // Get raw body as text\n      const rawBody = await c.req.text();\n\n      // Skip validation for empty bodies (e.g., GET requests with json content-type)\n      if (!rawBody || rawBody.trim() === \"\") {\n        return next();\n      }\n\n      // Quick check for obvious attacks before parsing\n      const structureCheck = validateJsonStructure(rawBody, {\n        maxDepth,\n        maxArrayLength,\n        maxObjectKeys,\n      });\n      if (!structureCheck.valid) {\n        securityLogger.logBlockedAttack(c, \"json_depth_protection\", {\n          reason: structureCheck.reason,\n          depth: structureCheck.depth,\n        });\n        return c.json({ error: `Invalid JSON structure: ${structureCheck.reason}` }, 413);\n      }\n\n      // Parse JSON to verify it's valid and do final depth check\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(rawBody);\n      const actualDepth = calculateJsonDepth(parsed, 0, {\n        maxDepth,\n        maxArrayLength,\n        maxObjectKeys,\n      });\n\n      if (actualDepth > maxDepth) {\n        securityLogger.logBlockedAttack(c, \"json_depth_protection\", {\n          reason: \"Maximum depth exceeded\",\n          depth: actualDepth,\n        });\n        return c.json({ error: `JSON depth exceeds maximum of ${maxDepth}` }, 413);\n      }\n\n      // Don't store the parsed body - let Hono's normal json() handle it\n      // This avoids consuming the body stream before downstream handlers can read it\n      await next();\n    } catch (error) {\n      // Re-throw HTTP exceptions (our own validation errors)\n      if (error instanceof HTTPException) {\n        throw error;\n error\",\n      });\n      return c.json({ error: \"Invalid JSON\" }, 400);\n    }\n  };\n}\n","numLines":284,"startLine":1,"totalLines":284}}}

* HTTP method restrictions middleware for Hono.\n *\n * OWASP A01:2021 - Broken Access Control\n * OWASP A05:2021 - Security Misconfiguration\n *\n * Restricts access to potentially dangerous HTTP methods that\n * could be used for reconnaissance or attacks.\n *\n * Dangerous methods blocked:\n * - TRACE: Can be used for Cross-Site Tracing (XST) attacks to steal cookies\n * - CONNECT: Can be used as a proxy for attacks on other servers\n * - PATCH: Often misconfigured, can lead to data integrity issues\n *\n * Safe methods allowed:\n * - GET, HEAD, OPTIONS: Read-only operations\n * - POST: Standard state-changing operations\n * - PUT: Resource updates (if needed by your API)\n * - DELETE: Resource deletion (if needed by your API)\n *\n * This middleware should be applied globally to all routes.\n */\n\nimport type { MiddlewareHandler } from \"hono\";\nimport { HTTPException } from \"hono/http-exception\";\nimport { securityLogger } from \"./security-logging.js\";\n\n/**\n * HTTP method restrictions options.\n */\ninterface HttpMethodRestrictionsOptions {\n  /** Additional allowed methods beyond the safe defaults (default: []) */\n  additionalAllowedMethods?: string[];\n  /** Additional blocked methods beyond the dangerous defaults (default: []) */\n  additionalBlockedMethods?: string[];\n  /** Skip validation for these paths (default: []) */\n  excludePaths?: string[];\n  /** Whether to log all method requests (default: false) */\n  logAllMethods?: boolean;\n  /** Custom error message (default: \"Method not allowed\") */\n  errorMessage?: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Safe HTTP methods that are always allowed.\n * These are read-only methods that don't modify server state.\n */\nconst SAFE_METHODS = [\"GET\", \"HEAD\", \"OPTIONS\"] as const;\n\n/**\n * Common state-changing methods that are typically allowed.\n * These are standard methods for REST APIs.\n */\nconst COMMON_STATE_CHANGING_METHODS = [\"POST\", \"PUT\", \"DELETE\", \"PATCH\"] as const;\n\n/**\n * Dangerous HTTP methods that should be blocked.\n * These methods can be used for attacks or reconnaissance.\n */\nconst DANGEROUS_METHODS = [\"TRACE\", \"CONNECT\"] as const;\n\n/**\n * Other potentially unsafe methods that should be explicitly allowed.\n */\nconst OTHER_METHODS = [\"PROPFIND\", \"PROPPATCH\", \"MKCOL\", \"COPY\", \"MOVE\", \"LOCK\", \"UNLOCK\"] as const;\n\n/**\n * HTTP method restrictions middleware.\n *\n * Blocks dangerous HTTP methods and logs the attempt.\n * Returns 405 Method Not Allowed for blocked methods.\n */\nexport function httpMethodRestrictions(\n  options: HttpMethodRestrictionsOptions = {}\n): MiddlewareHandler {\n  const {\n    additionalAllowedMethods = [],\n    additionalBlockedMethods = [],\n    excludePaths = [],\n    logAllMethods = false,\n    errorMessage = \"Method not allowed\",\n  } = options;\n\n  // Build the set of allowed methods\n  const allowedMethods = new Set<string>([\n    ...SAFE_METHODS,\n    ...COMMON_STATE_CHANGING_METHODS,\n    ...additionalAllowedMethods,\n  ]);\n\n  // Build the set of blocked methods (dangerous + additional)\n  const blockedMethods = new Set<string>([...DANGEROUS_METHODS, ...additionalBlockedMethods]);\n\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const method = c.req.method;\n    const path = c.req.path;\n\n    // Skip if path is excluded\n    if (excludePaths.some((exclude) => path.startsWith(exclude))) {\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // Log all method requests if enabled\n    if (logAllMethods) {\n      securityLogger.logSecurityEvent(c, \"http_method_request\", {\n        method,\n        path,\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Check if method is explicitly blocked\n    if (blockedMethods.has(method)) {\n      securityLogger.logBlockedAttack(c, \"http_method_blocked\", {\n        method,\n        path,\n      });\n\n      // Return 405 with Allow header showing valid methods\n      c.header(\"Allow\", Array.from(allowedMethods).join(\", \"));\n      c.header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain; charset=utf-8\");\n\n      throw new HTTPException(405, {\n        message: `${method} method is not allowed: ${errorMessage}`,\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Optionally warn about uncommon methods\n    if (OTHER_METHODS.includes(method as (typeof OTHER_METHODS)[number])) {\n      securityLogger.logSecurityEvent(c, \"http_method_uncommon\", {\n        method,\n        path,\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Add Allow header for OPTIONS requests\n    if (method === \"OPTIONS\") {\n      c.header(\"Allow\", Array.from(allowedMethods).join(\", \"));\n    }\n\n    await next();\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a method is safe (read-only).\n */\nexport function isSafeMethod(method: string): boolean {\n  return SAFE_METHODS.includes(method.toUpperCase() as (typeof SAFE_METHODS)[number]);\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a method is dangerous.\n */\nexport function isDangerousMethod(method: string): boolean {\n  return DANGEROUS_METHODS.includes(method.toUpperCase() as (typeof DANGEROUS_METHODS)[number]);\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a method is idempotent (safe + PUT/DELETE).\n * Idempotent methods can be safely retried.\n */\nexport function isIdempotentMethod(method: string): boolean {\n  return (\n    isSafeMethod(method) || method.toUpperCase() === \"PUT\" || method.toUpperCase() === \"DELETE\"\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the set of allowed methods.\n */\nexport function getAllowedMethods(additional: string[] = []): Set<string> {\n  return new Set([...SAFE_METHODS, ...COMMON_STATE_CHANGING_METHODS, ...additional]);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the set of blocked methods.\n */\nexport function getBlockedMethods(additional: string[] = []): Set<string> {\n  return new Set([...DANGEROUS_METHODS, ...additional]);\n}\n\n/**\n * Strict HTTP method restrictions middleware.\n *\n * Like httpMethodRestrictions but only allows GET, HEAD, OPTIONS, POST.\n * Blocks PUT, DELETE, PATCH unless explicitly allowed.\n * Use this for applications that don't need full REST API support.\n */\nexport function strictHttpMethodRestrictions(\n  options: Omit<HttpMethodRestrictionsOptions, \"additionalBlockedMethods\"> = {}\n): MiddlewareHandler {\n  const {\n    additionalAllowedMethods = [],\n    excludePaths = [],\n    logAllMethods = false,\n    errorMessage = \"Method not allowed\",\n  } = options;\n\n  // Only allow safe methods + POST\n  const allowedMethods = new Set([...SAFE_METHODS, \"POST\", ...additionalAllowedMethods]);\n\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    const method = c.req.method;\n    const path = c.req.path;\n\n    // Skip if path is excluded\n    if (excludePaths.some((exclude) => path.startsWith(exclude))) {\n      return next();\n    }\n\n    // Log all method requests if enabled\n    if (logAllMethods) {\n      securityLogger.logSecurityEvent(c, \"http_method_request\", {\n        method,\n        path,\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Check if method is allowed\n    if (!allowedMethods.has(method)) {\n      securityLogger.logBlockedAttack(c, \"http_method_blocked_strict\", {\n        method,\n        path,\n      });\n\n      c.header(\"Allow\", Array.from(allowedMethods).join(\", \"));\n      c.header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain; charset=utf-8\");\n\n      throw new HTTPException(405, {\n        message: `${method} method is not allowed: ${errorMessage}`,\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Add Allow header for OPTIONS requests\n    if (method === \"OPTIONS\") {\n      c.header(\"Allow\", Array.from(allowedMethods).join(\", \"));\n    }\n\n    await next();\n  };\n}\n","numLines":242,"startLine":1,"totalLines":242}}}

=> null),\n}));\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Response schemas for validation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst HealthResponseSchema = z.object({\n  status: z.enum([\"ok\", \"degraded\"]),\n  timestamp: z.string(),\n  uptime_seconds: z.number(),\n  feeds: z.array(\n    z.object({\n      id: z.string(),\n      name: z.string(),\n      status: z.enum([\"ok\", \"stale\", \"circuit_open\", \"never_polled\"]),\n      lastSuccessAt: z.string().nullable(),\n      lastPollAt: z.string().nullable(),\n      consecutiveFailures: z.number(),\n      entityCount: z.number().nullable(),\n      lastError: z.number(),\n  }),\n  delayDetector: z.object({\n    trackedTrips: z.number(),\n    activeAlerts: z.number(),\n    thresholdMultiplier: z.number(),\n    minTrainsForLineAlert: z.number(),\n  }),\n  equipment: z.object({\n    lastUpdated: z.string().nullable(),\n    outageCount: z.number(),\n    stationCount: z.number(),\n  }),\n  pushSubscriptions: z.number(),\n  cacheHitRate: z.number(),\n  memory: z.object({\n    rssBytes: z.number(),\n    heapUsedBytes: z.number(),\n    heapTotalBytes: z.number(),\n    externalBytes: z.number(),\n  }),\n  failingFeedsCount: z.number(),\n});\n\nconst StationSchema = z.object({\n  id: z.string(),\n  name: z.string(),\n  lat: z.number(),\n  lon: z.number(),\n  lines: z.array(z.string()),\n  northStopId: z.string(),\n  southStopId: z.string(),\n z.string(),\n  textColor: z.string(),\n  feedId: z.string(),\n  division: z.enum([\"A\", \"B\"]),\n  stops: z.array(z.string()),\n});\n\nconst AlertsResponseSchema = z.object({\n  alerts: z.array(\n    z.object({\n      id: z.string(),\n      severity: z.enum([\"info\", \"warning\", \"severe\"]),\n      source: z.enum([\"official\", \"predicted\"]),\n      headline: z.string(),\n      description: z.string(),\n  origin: z.object({\n    stationId: z.string(),\n    stationName: z.string(),\n  }),\n  destination: z.object({\n    stationId: z.string(),\n    stationName: z.string(),\n  }),\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction createTestApp(): Hono {\n  return createApp(STATIONS, ROUTES, COMPLEXES, TRANSFERS, \"/nonexistent/dist\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Helper to get a CSRF token from the test app.\n * Makes a GET request to /api/csrf-token and extracts the token.\n */\nasync function getCsrfToken(app: Hono): Promise<string> {\n  const res = await app.request(\"/api/csrf-token\");\n  expect(res.status).toBe(200);\n  const body = await res.json();\n  return body.token as string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Helper to make a state-changing request with CSRF token.\n * Gets a fresh token and includes it in the X-CSRF-Token header.\n */\nasync function _requestWithCsrf(\n  app: Hono,\n  path: string,\n  options: RequestInit = {}\n): Promise<Response> {\n  const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n  return app.request(path, {\n    ...options,\n    headers: {\n      ...options.headers,\n      \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n    },\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Helper to make an authenticated state-changing request with CSRF token.\n * Combines API key authentication with CSRF protection.\n */\nasync function requestWithAuthAndCsrf(\n  app: Hono,\n  path: string,\n  options: RequestInit = {}\n): Promise<Response> {\n  const credentials = await createTestUserCredentials();\n  const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n  return app.request(path, {\n    ...options,\n    headers: {\n      Authorization: credentials.authorizationHeader,\n      ...options.headers,\n      \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n    },\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Helper to make an authenticated admin request with CSRF token.\n * Uses admin credentials for operations that require elevated privileges.\n */\nasync function requestWithAdminAndCsrf(\n  app: Hono,\n  path: string,\n  options: RequestInit = {}\n): Promise<Response> {\n  const credentials = await createTestAdminCredentials();\n  const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n  return app.request(path, {\n    ...options,\n    headers: {\n      Authorization: credentials.authorizationHeader,\n      ...options.headers,\n      \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n    },\n  });\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe(\"API /api/health\", () => {\n  let app: Hono;\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    vi.clearAllMocks();\n    app = res.json();\n    const parsed = HealthResponseSchema.safeParse(body);\n    expect(parsed.success).toBe(true);\n    if (parsed.success) {\n      expect(parsed.data.status).toBe(\"ok\");\n      expect(parsed.data.feeds).toHaveLength(1);\n      expect(parsed.data.feeds[0].status).toBe(\"ok\");\n    }\n  });\n\n  it(\"includes feed latency metrics\", async () => {\n    const res = await app.request(\"/api/health\");\n    const body = await res.json();\n\n    expect(body.feeds[0].avgLatencyMs).toBe(105);\n    expect(body.feeds[0].errorCount24h).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  it(\"includes memory usage\", async () => {\n    const res = await app.request(\"/api/health\");\n    const body = await res.json();\n\n    expect(body.memory.rssBytes).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n   });\n\n    expect(res.status).toBe(200);\n  });\n});\n\ndescribe(\"Security headers\", () => {\n  let app: Hono;\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    app = createTestApp();\n  });\n\n  it(\"sets CSP headers
